River corridors and floodplains provide geographic contexts for tracing ecosystem energy flow and carrying capacity.
Agriculture and industry location comparisons often combine multiple weighted factors before choosing a region.
Runoff, precipitation, and evaporation process lines require function-graph reading before geographic interpretation.

topics.name: 人口与城市
topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 农业工业区位 after 人口与城市; it is the next topic in the junior_high geography learning sequence.
Population and city analysis should next connect people, labor, market, and land use to agricultural and industrial location factors.
Urban hierarchy and population concentration are commonly applied to explain transport networks, commuting, tourism, and regional flows.
Students often mix population or city distribution questions with broader regional-difference comparisons, so hints should separate the evidence used for each.
Population and urbanization charts train the same data-reading habits used in economic data graph interpretation.
Human population maps connect to biological population-growth curves through density, growth rate, and carrying-capacity reasoning.
Population structure, migration, and urbanization questions require reading statistical charts and trend graphs.

topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 自然灾害与防灾减灾 after 中国区域差异; it is the next topic in the junior_high geography learning sequence.
Regional comparison should start by locating boundaries, relative position, scale, and map symbols before explaining differences.
China regional differences are commonly applied to compare farming systems, industrial layout, and development conditions across regions.
Regional natural-human differences can be confused with population and urbanization patterns; diagnosis should ask which variable the question targets.

topics.name: 大气环流与天气系统
topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 水循环与水资源 after 大气环流与天气系统; it is the next topic in the senior_high geography learning sequence.
Weather-system problems first require reading isobars, wind direction, fronts, and spatial position from maps before explaining circulation.
Atmospheric circulation and weather systems are applied to explain rainstorms, typhoons, cold waves, drought, and disaster prevention scenarios.
Students often confuse short-term weather-system judgment with long-term climate-type classification, so feedback should contrast time scale and evidence.

topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 产业区位与区域发展 after 水循环与水资源; it is the next topic in the senior_high geography learning sequence.
After identifying precipitation, runoff, infiltration, and human intervention, learners can trace how water movement shapes erosion and deposition landforms.
Water-cycle reasoning is commonly applied to flood, drought, urban waterlogging, and water-resource management questions.
Water-cycle processes are often mixed with river-landform formation; diagnosis should separate circulation links from erosion and deposition results.

topics.name: 产业区位与区域发展
topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 地球运动 after 产业区位与区域发展; it is the next topic in the senior_high geography learning sequence.
Industrial-location evaluation should first list location factors such as resources, labor, market, transport, technology, policy, and environment.
Industrial location and industrial transfer are applied to evaluate regional transformation, carrying capacity, and sustainable development paths.
Transport conditions are one location factor, but students often treat transport influence as the whole industrial-location analysis.
Industrial transfer and site selection require comparing transport, labor, land, policy, and environmental costs against expected benefits.
Modern industrial-location patterns are easier to explain when linked to the historical rise of factories, transport networks, and urban labor markets.

topics.name: 自然灾害与防灾减灾
topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 交通运输与区域联系 after 自然灾害与防灾减灾; it is the next topic in the junior_high geography learning sequence.
Flood and drought questions should trace precipitation, runoff, infiltration, storage, and human activity before proposing mitigation measures.
Disaster analysis is applied to land-use planning, ecological restoration, resilient infrastructure, and regional sustainable development.
River landform formation can be confused with flood-disaster causation; feedback should distinguish normal geomorphic process from hazard exposure and vulnerability.
Regional sustainability comparisons depend on reading indicator tables, radar charts, and trend charts together.

topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Transport and trade questions should first read route direction, node location, distance, and regional connections from the map.
Transport accessibility and trade links are commonly applied to explain industrial layout, logistics cost, and regional economic ties.
Students often confuse transport effects with the full set of agricultural and industrial location factors, so review should separate factor type and conclusion.

topics.name: 地球运动
topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 外力作用与地貌 after 地球运动; it is the next topic in the senior_high geography learning sequence.
Earth-movement calculations should first read latitude, date, direction, and diagram labels before judging solar altitude and day length.
Seasonal solar-radiation differences help explain temperature belts, seasonal change, and some climate-distribution patterns.
Students may attribute seasonal day-length or solar-altitude changes to weather systems instead of Earth movement, so diagnosis should contrast mechanism and time scale.
Solar altitude, latitude, longitude, and time-zone calculations rely on angle measurement and angular distance.
Day-night alternation and seasonal change provide concrete contexts for periodic-function reasoning.

topics.name: 外力作用与地貌
topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Practice 区域可持续发展 after 外力作用与地貌; it is the next topic in the senior_high geography learning sequence.
Geomorphology problems can be confused with named river-landform recall unless students explain process, material, and time scale.
After explaining landform processes, students can evaluate land use, hazard exposure, and restoration choices in a region.
Slope, river, and coastal landform analysis supports disaster-risk questions about erosion, landslides, floods, and mitigation.

topics.name: 区域可持续发展
topics.subject: geography

related.reason: Regional sustainability problems often require explaining ecological restoration through community succession and habitat recovery.
Sustainable regional planning should compare ecological protection, growth, jobs, cost, and equity as explicit policy tradeoffs.
Regional sustainability is often confused with industrial location analysis unless ecological capacity and long-run governance are included.
